title: Survival 101
description: Your first day in a new world! Learn essential survival skills from crafting tools to farming — everything you need to thrive.
author: CraftMind

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Crafting Your First Tools
lessons.description: Punching trees with your bare hands? Let's fix that! Learn the crafting recipes for every essential tool.
objectives: Craft a crafting table and sticks

steps.description: Walk to the crafting tutorial area near spawn
Look at the giant crafting recipes displayed on the wall — each shows a tool pattern
Punch 3 oak logs from the marked trees, then convert them to 12 oak planks in your inventory
Craft a crafting table from 4 planks, place it down, and use it to make 8 sticks
Craft a wooden pickaxe (3 planks + 2 sticks), then mine 11 cobblestone to upgrade to stone tools
Craft stone versions of: pickaxe, axe, sword, and shovel. Arrange them in your hotbar!
Speed challenge: craft a full set of stone tools from scratch as fast as you can! ⛏️

quiz.question: What's the first thing you should craft after getting wood?
quiz.explanation: The crafting table unlocks nearly every recipe in the game! Without it, you're limited to the 2×2 crafting grid in your inventory.

quiz.question: How many planks do you get from 1 log?
quiz.explanation: Each oak log converts to 4 oak planks. So 3 logs = 12 planks — enough for a crafting table (4), 8 sticks (8), and a wooden pickaxe (3).

quiz.question: Which tool tier is fastest for mining stone?
quiz.explanation: Stone pickaxes (and above) can actually mine stone, cobblestone, and ores. Wooden and gold pickaxes are too slow or can't mine certain blocks at all!

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Finding & Building Shelter
lessons.description: Night falls fast and the monsters come out! Learn to find or build shelter quickly before the first night.
objectives: Build a quick dirt hut in under 2 minutes
Add a door and basic lighting

steps.description: Explore the area around spawn to find shelter candidates
Look at the three shelter options demonstrated: a hillside dig-in, a tree platform, and a small hut
Choose one spot and build a 3×3×3 dirt hut — it doesn't need to be pretty, it needs to be FAST!
Add an oak door to your hut and place a torch inside (prevents mob spawns)
Talk to ProfBlock about which shelter type is best for different situations
Improve your hut: replace dirt walls with cobblestone, add a window, and make it feel cozy! 🏡

quiz.question: What light level prevents hostile mobs from spawning?
quiz.explanation: Hostile mobs require a light level of 7 or darker to spawn. Torches produce light level 14, so placing one every 7 blocks keeps everything lit!

quiz.question: Which mobs can break down wooden doors?
quiz.explanation: Zombies on Hard difficulty can break down wooden doors! On Normal they'll bang on them but can't break through. Use iron doors for extra safety.

quiz.question: What's the fastest shelter you can build on day 1?
quiz.explanation: A dirt hut or a hole dug into a hillside is the fastest shelter — no crafting required! Just pile up dirt blocks or dig 3 blocks into a cliff face.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Surviving the First Night
lessons.description: The sun is setting. Do you know what to do? Learn the dangers of night and how to stay alive until dawn.
objectives: Recognize hostile mobs and their threats
Know when to fight vs when to hide

steps.description: Enter the night simulation arena
Watch the mob showcase — each hostile mob is displayed behind glass with a sign explaining its behavior
Zombie combat practice: fight 3 zombies in the arena using your sword. Strafe and attack!
Skeleton practice: skeletons shoot arrows! Use blocks as cover, then rush in close
Discuss creeper safety with ProfBlock — learn the 4-block safe distance rule
Survive the full night simulation (5 minutes) in the arena! You've got this! 🌙

quiz.question: What's the safest distance from a creeper before its explosion won't hurt you?
quiz.explanation: A creeper's explosion has a blast radius of about 3-4 blocks. If you're 5+ blocks away when it detonates, you'll take little to no damage. Sprint away!

quiz.question: Which mob drops an item useful for brewing?
quiz.explanation: Spiders drop spider eyes (for potions of poison), creepers drop gunpowder (for splash potions and fire charges), skeletons drop bones (for bone meal). Blazes drop blaze rods — essential for brewing!

quiz.question: What should you do if you see a skeleton at night with no weapons?
quiz.explanation: Without a weapon, skeletons will shoot you full of arrows! Your best option is to sprint away and get behind cover or into shelter.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Farming Fundamentals
lessons.description: Hunting is unpredictable — farming is forever! Learn to grow wheat, carrots, and breed animals for a reliable food supply.
objectives: Set up automatic watering with water channels

steps.description: Walk to the farm plots near the river
Examine the demonstration farm — notice the tilled soil near water, the crops at different growth stages, and the animal pen
Use a hoe to till a 9×9 plot of dirt. Place a water source in the center block for maximum hydration
Plant wheat seeds in the tilled soil — they'll grow faster near water! Hold seeds and right-click the dirt
Build a simple animal pen: fence in a 5×5 area, add a gate, and put 2 cows inside with some wheat
Feed wheat to both cows to breed them — a baby cow will appear! Heart particles mean it worked 💕
Expand your farm! Add a carrot patch, a beetroot section, and set up a composter. You're a real farmer now! 🌾

quiz.question: How close does farmland need to be to water to stay hydrated?
quiz.explanation: Water hydrates farmland up to 4 blocks away (Manhattan distance). That's why a single water block in the center of a 9×9 farm keeps all 80 plots hydrated!

quiz.question: What happens if you jump on fully-grown wheat?
quiz.explanation: Jumping on farmland (or dropping on it from height) can trample it — turning it back to dirt and destroying the crop! Be careful around your farms.

quiz.question: What do you feed cows to breed them?
quiz.explanation: Cows, sheep, and mooshrooms all eat wheat for breeding. Pigs eat carrots, potatoes, or beetroots. Chickens eat any seeds!

quiz.question: Why is farming better than hunting for food?
quiz.explanation: Farming is reliable and renewable — crops grow back, animals can be bred infinitely. Hunting depends on finding animals, which may not always be around, especially at night.
